Patrick's defense argued Elijah's vehicle swerved toward him, that he acted in self-defense, and that Patrick suffered from post-traumatic stress disorder after being struck while riding a bicycle in Chicago in 2019. Prosecutors cited Patrick's road rage history, three incidents within about a year before the shooting. In one, while riding a scooter, he knocked on the trunk of a vehicle blocking a bike lane, and when the driver stepped out, reports say he pulled a gun on her. As she raised her hands and backed away, he allegedly said, I ought to kill your ass before police got involved. Just a month prior to the shooting, he allegedly broke off a vehicle's side mirror and racked the slide on his handgun after allegedly being cut off. Prosecutors also refuted the self-defense claim, arguing Patrick shot at the car as it drove away. In February of 2025, Patrick was found guilty on all counts.
